title: viola heterophylla subsp. graeca
intro: Viola heterophylla subsp. graeca, also known as the Greek pansy or Graecian violet, is a perennial plant species belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Viola heterophylla subsp. graeca include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the dried leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
